description: Rohde & Schwarz SME family synthesized RF signal generator (SME02 5 kHz to 1.5 GHz, SME03/SME03E to 2.2/3 GHz, SME06 to 6 GHz). SCPI-controlled over the IEC/IEEE bus (GPIB) and RS-232. Provides CW/fixed and sweep/list frequency and level operation, automatic level control (ALC) and user level correction, analog modulation (AM, FM, PhiM, pulse modulation and an internal pulse generator), and option-dependent LF generators. This definition covers the core SCPI control surface: common * commands, ABORt, CALibration, DIAGnostic, DISPlay, FORMat, MEMory, OUTPut/OUTPut2, SOURce FREQuency/POWer/AM/FM/PM/PULM/PULSe/CORRection/PHASe/ROSCillator/SWEep, the SOURce0|2 LF generator subsystems, STATus, SYSTem, TEST, TRIGger and UNIT. Option-specific complex/aviation modulation subsystems (DM, ERMes, FLEX, POCSag, REFLex25, ILS, VOR, MBEacon, STEReo) are documented in the manual but only summarized here.

identity.description: Identification query. R&S SME returns a string such as 'Rohde&Schwarz,SME03,00000001,1.03' (manufacturer, model with variant id, serial number, firmware version).

commands.name: idn_query
commands.description: Identification query. Returns manufacturer, model/variant, serial number and firmware version, e.g. 'Rohde&Schwarz, SME03,00000001, 1.03'.

commands.name: clear_status
commands.description: CLEAR STATUS. Sets the status byte (STB), the standard event status register (ESR) and the EVENt parts of the QUEStionable and OPERation registers to zero, and clears the output buffer. Does not alter mask/transition parts. No query.

commands.name: event_status_enable
commands.description: EVENT STATUS ENABLE. Sets the event status enable register to the indicated value. Query *ESE? returns the register contents in decimal form.

commands.name: event_status_register_query
commands.description: STANDARD EVENT STATUS QUERY. Returns the contents of the event status register in decimal form (0 to 255) and then sets the register to zero. Query only.

commands.name: individual_status_query
commands.description: INDIVIDUAL STATUS QUERY. Returns the IST flag (status bit sent during a parallel poll) in decimal form (0 | 1). Query only.

commands.name: operation_complete
commands.description: OPERATION COMPLETE. Sets bit 0 in the event status register when all preceding commands have been executed; can be used to initiate a service request. Query *OPC? returns 1 once preceding commands are complete.

commands.name: option_query
commands.description: OPTION IDENTIFICATION QUERY. Returns a comma-separated list of installed options, each in a fixed position (e.g. SM-B1 OCXO, SM-B2 LF generator, SM-B3/B8/B9 pulse modulators, SM-B4 pulse generator, SM-B5 FM/PM modulator, SM-B6 multifunction generator, SME-B11 DM coder, SME-B12 DM memory extension). Query only.

commands.name: parallel_poll_enable
commands.description: PARALLEL POLL REGISTER ENABLE. Sets the parallel poll enable register to the indicated value. Query *PRE? returns its contents in decimal form.

commands.name: power_on_status_clear
commands.description: POWER ON STATUS CLEAR. Determines whether the ENABle register contents are maintained (0) or reset (non-zero) at power-on. Query *PSC? reads the power-on-status-clear flag (0 or 1).

commands.name: recall
commands.description: RECALL. Calls the instrument state previously stored under the given number with *SAV. Up to 50 states. No query.

commands.name: reset
commands.description: RESET. Sets the instrument to a defined default state (essentially as the PRESET key) except that the RF output is deactivated after *RST. No query.

commands.name: save
commands.description: SAVE. Stores the current instrument state under the indicated number (recalled with *RCL). No query.

commands.name: service_request_enable
commands.description: SERVICE REQUEST ENABLE. Sets the service request enable register to the indicated value (bit 6 MSS mask bit stays 0). Query *SRE? reads its contents in decimal form.

commands.name: status_byte_query
commands.description: READ STATUS BYTE QUERY. Reads the contents of the status byte in decimal form. Query only.

commands.name: trigger
commands.description: TRIGGER. Triggers all actions waiting for a trigger event (see the TRIGger system). No query.

commands.name: self_test_query
commands.description: SELF TEST QUERY. Triggers all instrument selftests and outputs an error code in decimal form (0 = pass). Query only.

commands.name: wait_to_continue
commands.description: WAIT-to-CONTINUE. Permits servicing of subsequent commands only after all preceding commands have been executed and all signals have settled.

commands.name: abort_sweep
commands.description: Aborts a running sweep. Triggers an event (no *RST value). After aborting it can be triggered again at once.

commands.name: abort_list
commands.description: Aborts a list execution.

commands.name: abort_msequence
commands.description: Aborts a memory sequence.

commands.name: abort_dm
commands.description: Aborts the single execution of a DM list (relates to the digital modulation type set with SOURce:DM:TYPE). Option SME-B11.

commands.name: abort_xmem
commands.description: Aborts recording of data into the DM memory extension list 'XMEM'. Option SME-B12.

commands.name: calibration_qpsk_data
commands.description: Enters the QPSK modulator calibration data (a delay for the digital FM signal). DEFault uses the value saved in FLASH memory. Option SME-B11.

commands.name: calibration_qpsk_store
commands.description: Saves the QPSK delay presently set under [:DATA] as a default value in FLASH memory.

commands.name: calibration_level_data_query
commands.description: Queries the level correction data in the format set in the FORMat system. The list returned is determined by :FRANge and :PMODulator. Query only.

commands.name: calibration_level_frange
commands.description: Selects the level correction table valid for a frequency in the NORMal or MIXer range (for the DATA? readout only; does not affect the instrument setup). *RST value NORMal.

commands.name: calibration_level_pmodulator
commands.description: Selects the level correction table valid for an instrument setup with the pulse modulator ON or OFF (for DATA? readout only). *RST value OFF.

commands.name: calibration_level_state
commands.description: Switches internal level correction on or off. *RST value ON.

commands.name: calibration_lpreset_measure
commands.description: Triggers a level-preset calibration measurement. Returns 0 on success. Query only.

commands.name: calibration_lpreset_data_query
commands.description: Queries the level-preset correction data in the format set in the FORMat system. Query only.

commands.name: calibration_pulse_measure
commands.description: Triggers a pulse generator calibration measurement. Returns 0 on success. Option SM-B4. Query only.

commands.name: calibration_pulse_data_query
commands.description: Queries the pulse generator correction data as two comma-separated integers (fine adjustment, coarse adjustment). Query only.

commands.name: calibration_roscillator_data
commands.description: Enters the reference oscillator correction data.

commands.name: calibration_vsummation_measure
commands.description: Triggers a calibration measurement to determine the support values for frequency setting. Returns 0 on success. Query only.

commands.name: diagnostic_ccount_attenuator_query
commands.description: Queries the number of switching processes of attenuator stage n (Z1..Z6: 40/20/5/20/10/40 dB). Query only.

commands.name: diagnostic_ccount_power_query
commands.description: Queries the number of switch-on processes (power cycles). Query only.

commands.name: diagnostic_modules_query
commands.description: Queries the modules in the instrument with their variant and revision numbers, returned as a comma-separated list. Query only.

commands.name: diagnostic_otime_query
commands.description: Reads the internal operating-hours counter (number of hours of operation). Query only.

commands.name: diagnostic_sdate_query
commands.description: Queries the date of software creation in the form year, month, day. Query only.

commands.name: diagnostic_measure_point_query
commands.description: Triggers a measurement at a measuring point (specified by the numeric suffix) and returns the measured voltage. Query only.

commands.name: display_annotation_all
commands.description: Switches the frequency and amplitude indication on or off. ANNotation ON can only be executed if SYSTem:SECurity is OFF. *RST value ON (with security OFF).

commands.name: display_annotation_amplitude
commands.description: Switches the amplitude indication on or off (requires SYSTem:SECurity OFF to switch ON). *RST value ON.

commands.name: display_annotation_frequency
commands.description: Switches the frequency indication on or off (requires SYSTem:SECurity OFF to switch ON). *RST value ON.

commands.name: format_data
commands.description: Specifies the data format the SME uses for returning data: ASCii (plain text, comma separated) or PACKed (binary block data). *RST value ASCii.

commands.name: format_border
commands.description: Defines the byte order inside IEEE-754 binary blocks: NORMal (MSB first, matches 80x86 hosts) or SWAPped (LSB first). *RST value NORMal.

commands.name: memory_nstates_query
commands.description: Returns the number of *SAV/*RCL memories available (50 total). Query only.

commands.name: output_amode
commands.description: Switches the attenuator operating mode at the RF output. AUTO switches the attenuator whenever possible; FIXed switches only when certain fixed levels are crossed. *RST value AUTO.

commands.name: output_blank_polarity
commands.description: Sets the polarity of the BLANk signal. NORMal: active BLANk = higher output voltage; INVerted: active BLANk = lower output voltage. *RST value NORMal.

commands.name: output_impedance_query
commands.description: Queries the RF output impedance (fixed 50 Ohm), enabling conversion of level between V and W. Query only.

commands.name: output_protection_clear
commands.description: Resets the RF output overvoltage protective circuit after it has tripped; the output state is then again determined by OUTPut:STATe. Triggers an event (no *RST value).

commands.name: output_protection_tripped_query
commands.description: Queries the state of the RF output protective circuit (0 = not responded, 1 = responded). Query only.

commands.name: output_state
commands.description: Switches the RF output on or off. *RST sets this to OFF (output deactivated), unlike the PRESET key. The protective circuit can also switch the output off without changing this parameter.

commands.name: output_state_pon
commands.description: Selects the RF output state after power-on. OFF: output switched off; UNCHanged: same state as before switch-off. *RST does not affect this setting.

commands.name: output2_source
commands.description: Selects which LF generator is connected to the LF output socket: 0 = LF generator 1, 2 = LF generator 2 (options SM-B2 and SM-B6). *RST value 0.

commands.name: output2_source_stereo
commands.description: Determines whether the complete stereo multiplex signal (MPX) or only the pilot tone (PILot) is output (LF generator 2 in STEREO mode, option SM-B6). *RST value MPX.

commands.name: output2_state
commands.description: Switches the LF output on or off. *RST value OFF.

commands.name: output2_voltage
commands.description: Sets the LF output voltage (a characteristic of the output, kept even if another LF generator is connected). *RST value 1 V.

commands.name: frequency_cw
commands.description: Sets the RF frequency for CW operation (coupled to the current sweep frequency). UP/DOWN increment/decrement by FREQuency:STEP. Specified range applies for OFFSet = 0. *RST value 100 MHz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 5 kHz to 1.5 GHz (SME03E/03/06: to 2.2/3/6 GHz); UP | DOWN

commands.name: frequency_cw_rcl
commands.description: Determines whether a saved frequency is loaded (INCLude) or the current setting kept (EXCLude) when instrument settings are recalled. *RST has no effect on this setting.

commands.name: frequency_center
commands.description: Sets the sweep center frequency (coupled to STARt and STOP). OFFSet is considered. *RST value (STARt+STOP)/2.

commands.name: frequency_manual
commands.description: Sets the frequency when SWEep:MODE MANual and FREQuency:MODE SWEep are set; only values between STARt and STOP are permitted. *RST value 100 MHz.

commands.name: frequency_mode
commands.description: Specifies the frequency operating mode. CW/FIXed: output from FREQuency:CW. SWEep: from STARt/STOP/CENTer/SPAN/MANual. LIST: processes a frequency/level list (also sets POWer:MODE LIST). *RST value CW.

commands.name: frequency_offset
commands.description: Sets the frequency offset of a series-connected device (e.g. mixer): displayed frequency = RF output frequency + OFFSet. Entering an offset changes only the query value, not the RF output frequency. *RST value 0.

commands.name: frequency_span
commands.description: Sets the sweep frequency span (coupled to STARt/STOP/CENTer). Negative values allowed (then STARt > STOP). STARt = CENTer - SPAN/2, STOP = CENTer + SPAN/2. *RST value (STOP-STARt).

commands.name: frequency_start
commands.description: Sets the start frequency of the sweep (STARt may be larger than STOP). Coupled to STOP/SPAN/CENTer. *RST value 100 MHz.

commands.name: frequency_stop
commands.description: Sets the stop frequency of the sweep (may be smaller than STARt). *RST value 500 MHz.

commands.name: frequency_step
commands.description: Sets the step width used when frequency UP/DOWN is given (coupled to the KNOB STEP in manual control). Linear steps only. *RST value 1 MHz.

commands.name: power_level
commands.description: Sets the RF output level in CW mode. UP/DOWN increment/decrement by POWer:STEP. OFFSet is considered (range valid for OFFSet=0). Units other than dBm allowed (e.g. V) via UNIT:POWer. *RST value -30 dBm.

commands.name: power_offset
commands.description: Sets the constant level offset of a series-connected attenuator/amplifier: displayed level = RF output level + OFFSet. Only dB allowed (no linear units). Offset also applies to level sweeps. *RST value 0.

commands.name: power_rcl
commands.description: Determines whether a saved RF level is loaded (INCLude) or the current setting kept (EXCLude) on recall. *RST has no effect.

commands.name: power_alc_bandwidth
commands.description: Sets the bandwidth of the automatic level control. *RST value 100 kHz.

commands.name: power_alc_bandwidth_auto
commands.description: Determines ALC bandwidth adaptation: ON automatic, OFF none, ONCE adapt once then set AUTO OFF. *RST value ON.

commands.name: power_alc_state
commands.description: Switches automatic level control on or off. ON: ALC permanently on; OFF: ALC switched on briefly only when the level changes. *RST value ON.

commands.name: power_limit
commands.description: Limits the maximum RF output level in CW and SWEEP modes. Does not affect the LEVEL display or the response to POW?. *RST value +16 dBm.

commands.name: power_manual
commands.description: Sets the level when POWer:MODE SWEep and SWEep:MODE MANual are set; only values between START and STOP permitted. *RST value -30 dBm.

commands.name: power_mode
commands.description: Specifies the level operating mode. FIXed: from POWer:LEVel. SWEep: from POWer STARt/STOP/MANual. LIST: processes a frequency/level list (also sets FREQuency:MODE LIST). *RST value FIXed.

commands.name: power_start
commands.description: Sets the start value for a level sweep (STARt may be larger than STOP, sweeping high to low). *RST value -30 dBm.

commands.name: power_stop
commands.description: Sets the final value for a level sweep (STOP may be smaller than STARt). *RST value -10 dBm.

commands.name: power_step
commands.description: Sets the step width for level UP/DOWN (coupled to KNOB STEP). Only dB allowed (no linear units). *RST value 1 dB.

commands.name: correction_state
commands.description: Switches the user-correction (UCOR) table selected with CORRection:CSET on or off. *RST value OFF.

commands.name: correction_cset_catalog_query
commands.description: Requests a comma-separated list of UCOR tables. Query only.

commands.name: correction_cset_select
commands.description: Selects (or creates if non-existent) a UCOR table by name (up to 7 letters). Activation requires CORRection:STATe ON. Triggers an event (no *RST value).

commands.name: correction_cset_data_frequency
commands.description: Transmits the frequency points for the selected UCOR table (comma-separated list). *RST does not influence data lists.

parameters.name: frequency_list
parameters.range: 5 kHz to 1.5 GHz each (SME03E/03/06: to 2.2/3/6 GHz)

commands.name: correction_cset_data_power
commands.description: Transmits the level correction values for the selected UCOR table (comma-separated list). *RST does not influence data lists.

commands.name: correction_cset_delete
commands.description: Deletes the named UCOR table from instrument memory. Triggers an event (no *RST value).

commands.name: am_depth
commands.description: Sets the amplitude modulation depth in percent. *RST value 30 PCT.

commands.name: am_external_coupling
commands.description: Selects the coupling of the external AM input. AC removes the DC content; DC leaves the signal unaltered. *RST value AC.

commands.name: am_internal_frequency
commands.description: Sets the internal AM modulation frequency. INT1 = LF generator 1, INT2 = LF generator 2. Without SM-B2/SM-B6 only 400 Hz/1 kHz/3 kHz/15 kHz; with SM-B2 0.1 Hz-500 kHz; with SM-B6 0.1 Hz-1 MHz. *RST value 1 kHz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 400 Hz | 1 kHz | 3 kHz | 15 kHz, or 0.1 Hz to 500 kHz (SM-B2), or 0.1 Hz to 1 MHz (SM-B6)

commands.name: am_polarity
commands.description: Selects the AM polarity. NORMal: positive modulation voltage gives higher output level; INVerted inverts. *RST value NORMal.

commands.name: am_source
commands.description: Selects the AM modulation source(s). INT1 = LF generator 1, INT2 = LF generator 2 (option SM-B2/SM-B6). An external and an internal source may be given together. *RST value INT1.

commands.name: am_state
commands.description: Switches amplitude modulation on or off. *RST value OFF.

commands.name: fm_deviation
commands.description: Sets the FM frequency deviation. Two independent modulators FM1/FM2 (option SM-B5). Max deviation depends on SOURce:FREQuency. *RST value 10 kHz.

parameters.name: deviation
parameters.range: 0 to 1 MHz (SME03/03E: 0 to 2 MHz; SME06: 0 to 4 MHz)

commands.name: fm_external_coupling
commands.description: Selects the coupling of the external FM input (socket selected by the suffix after EXTernal). AC removes DC content; DC leaves signal unaltered. *RST value AC.

commands.name: fm_internal_frequency
commands.description: Sets the internal FM modulation frequency (FM1 uses LF generator 1, FM2 LF generator 2). Without SM-B2/SM-B6 only 400 Hz/1 kHz/3 kHz/15 kHz; with SM-B2 0.1 Hz-500 kHz; with SM-B6 0.1 Hz-1 MHz. *RST value 1 kHz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 400 Hz | 1 kHz | 3 kHz | 15 kHz, or 0.1 Hz to 500 kHz (SM-B2), or 0.1 Hz to 1 MHz (SM-B6)

commands.name: fm_preemphasis
commands.description: Selects the FM preemphasis: 0 (none), 50us (European standard) or 75us (American standard). *RST value 0.

commands.name: fm_source
commands.description: Selects the FM modulation source. LF generator 1 = INT for FM1, LF generator 2 for FM2 (option SM-B5). Several sources can be active simultaneously. *RST value FM1: INT, FM2: EXT2.

commands.name: fm_state
commands.description: Switches frequency modulation on or off. *RST value OFF.

commands.name: pm_deviation
commands.description: Sets the phase modulation deviation in radians (DEGree accepted). Two independent modulators PM1/PM2 (option SM-B5). *RST value 1 rad.

commands.name: pm_external_coupling
commands.description: Selects the coupling of the external PM input (socket selected by the suffix after EXTernal). AC removes DC content; DC leaves signal unchanged. *RST value AC.

commands.name: pm_internal_frequency
commands.description: Sets the internal PM modulation frequency (PM1 uses LF generator 1, PM2 LF generator 2). Without SM-B2/SM-B6 only 400 Hz/1 kHz/3 kHz/15 kHz; with SM-B2 0.1 Hz-500 kHz; with SM-B6 0.1 Hz-1 MHz. *RST value 1 kHz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 400 Hz | 1 kHz | 3 kHz | 15 kHz, or 0.1 Hz to 500 kHz (SM-B2), or 0.1 Hz to 1 MHz (SM-B6)

commands.name: pm_source
commands.description: Selects the PM modulation source. LF generator 1 = INTernal for PM1, LF generator 2 for PM2 (option SM-B5). Several sources can be active simultaneously. *RST value PM1: INT, PM2: EXT2.

commands.name: pm_state
commands.description: Switches the phase modulation (selected by the suffix after PM) on or off. *RST value OFF.

commands.name: pulm_external_impedance
commands.description: Sets the impedance of the external pulse modulation input socket (independent of PM/FM input settings). Options SM-B3/SM-B4/SM-B8/SM-B9. *RST value 10 kOhm.

commands.name: pulm_internal_frequency
commands.description: Sets the internal pulse generator frequency (coupled to PULSe:PERiod). *RST value 100 kHz.

commands.name: pulm_polarity
commands.description: Sets the polarity between modulating and modulated pulse signal. NORMal: RF suppressed during interpulse period; INVerted: RF suppressed during the pulse. *RST value NORMal.

commands.name: pulm_source
commands.description: Selects the pulse modulation source. INTernal: internal pulse generator (option SM-B4); EXTernal: externally fed signal. *RST value INTernal.

commands.name: pulm_state
commands.description: Switches pulse modulation on or off. *RST value OFF.

commands.name: pulse_delay
commands.description: Sets the time from the start of the period to the first pulse edge (option SM-B4). Forced to 0 while DOUBle:STATe is ON. *RST value 1 us.

commands.name: pulse_double_delay
commands.description: Sets the time from the start of the pulse period to the first edge of the second (double) pulse. *RST value 1 us.

commands.name: pulse_double_state
commands.description: Switches the second pulse on or off. When ON, PULSe:DELay is set to 0 and cannot be changed. *RST value OFF.

commands.name: pulse_period
commands.description: Sets the pulse period (reciprocal of pulse frequency; coupled to PULM:INTernal:FREQuency). *RST value 10 us.

commands.name: pulse_width
commands.description: Sets the pulse width. *RST value 1 us.

commands.name: phase_adjust
commands.description: Sets the phase between the output signal and the reference oscillator signal (RADian or DEGree). Accepted only by PHASe:REFerence. *RST value 0.0 DEG.

commands.name: phase_reference
commands.description: Accepts the phase set with PHASe:ADJust as the new reference phase. Triggers an event (no *RST value).

commands.name: roscillator_external_frequency
commands.description: Informs the instrument of the external reference oscillator frequency. *RST value 10 MHz.

commands.name: roscillator_internal_adjust_state
commands.description: Switches fine adjustment (frequency correction) of the internal reference on or off. *RST value OFF.

commands.name: roscillator_internal_adjust_value
commands.description: Sets the internal reference frequency correction (tuning) value. *RST value 2048.

commands.name: roscillator_source
commands.description: Selects the reference source: INTernal (internal oscillator) or EXTernal (externally fed reference). *RST value INTernal.

commands.name: sweep_btime
commands.description: Sets the sweep blank time (valid for all sweeps including LF). NORMal: shortest possible; LONG: long enough for an XY recorder to return to 0. *RST value NORMal.

commands.name: sweep_frequency_dwell
commands.description: Sets the dwell time per frequency step. *RST value 15 ms.

commands.name: sweep_frequency_mode
commands.description: Specifies the frequency sweep run. AUTO: each trigger one full sweep; MANual: each step by manual control / FREQuency:MANual; STEP: each trigger one step. *RST value AUTO.

commands.name: sweep_frequency_points
commands.description: Determines the number of steps in a frequency sweep (coupled to SPAN and STEP). STEP:LINear / STEP:LOGarithmic are preferred over POINts.

commands.name: sweep_frequency_spacing
commands.description: Selects linear or logarithmic step spacing for the frequency sweep. *RST value LINear.

commands.name: sweep_frequency_step_linear
commands.description: Sets the step width for a linear frequency sweep (adapts POINts for SPACing LINear). *RST value 1 MHz.

commands.name: sweep_frequency_step_logarithmic
commands.description: Sets the step width factor (percent of prior frequency) for a logarithmic frequency sweep. Suffix PCT must be used explicitly. *RST value 1 PCT.

commands.name: sweep_power_dwell
commands.description: Sets the dwell time per level step. *RST value 15 ms.

commands.name: sweep_power_mode
commands.description: Specifies the level sweep run. AUTO: each trigger one full sweep; MANual: each step by manual control / POWer:MANual; STEP: each trigger one step. *RST value AUTO.

commands.name: sweep_power_points
commands.description: Determines the number of steps in a level sweep (coupled to SPAN and STEP). STEP:LOGarithmic is preferred over POINts.

commands.name: sweep_power_step_logarithmic
commands.description: Sets the step width factor for a logarithmic level sweep (suffix dB must be used explicitly). *RST value 1 dB.

commands.name: lfgen_frequency_cw
commands.description: Sets the LF generator CW frequency. SOURce0 = standard/LF gen 1, SOURce2 = LF gen 2. Without SM-B2/SM-B6 only 400 Hz/1 kHz/3 kHz/15 kHz; with SM-B2 0.1 Hz-500 kHz; with SM-B6 0.1 Hz-1 MHz. *RST value 1 kHz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 400 Hz | 1 kHz | 3 kHz | 15 kHz, or 0.1 Hz to 500 kHz (SM-B2), or 0.1 Hz to 1 MHz (SM-B6)

commands.name: lfgen_frequency_manual
commands.description: Sets the LF generator frequency when SOURce2:SWEep:MODE MANual and SOURce2:FREQuency:MODE SWEep are set; only values between STARt and STOP allowed. *RST value 1 kHz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 0.1 Hz to 500 kHz (SM-B2) or 0.1 Hz to 1 MHz (SM-B6)

commands.name: lfgen_frequency_mode
commands.description: Specifies the LF generator operating mode. CW/FIXed: output from SOURce0|2:FREQuency:CW. SWEep (only for SOURce2): from STARt/STOP/MANual. *RST value CW.

commands.name: lfgen_frequency_start
commands.description: Sets the start value of the LF generator sweep frequency (SOURce2). *RST value 1 kHz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 0.1 Hz to 500 kHz (SM-B2) or 0.1 Hz to 1 MHz (SM-B6)

commands.name: lfgen_frequency_stop
commands.description: Sets the end value of the LF generator sweep frequency (SOURce2). *RST value 100 kHz.

parameters.name: frequency
parameters.range: 0.1 Hz to 500 kHz (SM-B2) or 0.1 Hz to 1 MHz (SM-B6)

commands.name: lfgen_function_shape
commands.description: Specifies the LF output waveform. Standard generator is sine only; option SM-B2 adds SQUare/TRIangle/PRNoise; option SM-B6 adds all shapes including SAWTooth. *RST value SINusoid.

commands.name: lfgen_marker_aoff
commands.description: Switches off all LF frequency-sweep markers. Triggers an event (no *RST value, no query).

commands.name: lfgen_marker_frequency
commands.description: Sets the LF frequency-sweep marker (selected by suffix 1|2|3) to the indicated frequency. *RST values MARK1: 100 kHz, MARK2: 10 kHz, MARK3: 1 kHz.

commands.name: lfgen_marker_state
commands.description: Switches the LF frequency-sweep marker (selected by suffix 1|2|3) on or off. *RST value OFF.

commands.name: lfgen_marker_polarity
commands.description: Sets the LF marker signal polarity. NORMal: TTL level on marker condition, 0 V otherwise; INVerted: opposite. *RST value NORMal.

commands.name: status_operation_event_query
commands.description: Queries (and clears) the EVENt part of the STATus:OPERation register. Query only.

commands.name: status_operation_condition_query
commands.description: Queries the CONDition part of the STATus:OPERation register (not cleared on read; reflects current hardware state). Query only.

commands.name: status_operation_ptransition
commands.description: Sets the positive-transition edge detectors (0->1) of the STATus:OPERation register.

commands.name: status_operation_ntransition
commands.description: Sets the negative-transition edge detectors (1->0) of the STATus:OPERation register.

commands.name: status_operation_enable
commands.description: Sets the ENABle part of the STATus:OPERation register, enabling individual events for the summary bit in the status byte.

commands.name: status_preset
commands.description: Resets the edge detectors and ENABle parts of all registers to a defined value (PTRansition = FFFFh, NTRansition = 0, ENABle of OPERation/QUEStionable = 0). No query.

commands.name: status_questionable_event_query
commands.description: Queries (and clears) the EVENt part of the STATus:QUEStionable register. Query only.

commands.name: status_questionable_condition_query
commands.description: Queries the CONDition part of the STATus:QUEStionable register (not cleared on read). Query only.

commands.name: status_questionable_ptransition
commands.description: Sets the positive-transition edge detectors (0->1) of the STATus:QUEStionable register.

commands.name: status_questionable_ntransition
commands.description: Sets the negative-transition edge detectors (1->0) of the STATus:QUEStionable register.

commands.name: status_questionable_enable
commands.description: Sets the ENABle part of the STATus:QUEStionable register, enabling individual events for the summary bit in the status byte.

commands.name: status_queue_next_query
commands.description: Queries and deletes the oldest entry in the error queue. Returns 0,"No error" if empty. Identical to SYSTem:ERRor?. Query only.

commands.name: system_beeper_state
commands.description: Switches the beeper on or off. *RST value OFF.

commands.name: system_gpib_address
commands.description: Sets the IEC/IEEE-bus (GPIB) instrument address. *RST value 28.

commands.name: system_serial_baud
commands.description: Sets the RS-232 baud rate for transmit and receive (data format fixed 8N1). *RST has no influence; *RST value 9600.

commands.name: system_serial_control_rts
commands.description: Sets the RS-232 hardware handshake. ON: RTS always active; IBFull/RFR: RTS active while ready to receive. *RST value RFR.

commands.name: system_serial_pace
commands.description: Sets the RS-232 software handshake. XON: XON/XOFF (ASCII 11h/13h); NONE: no software handshake. *RST value NONE.

commands.name: system_error_query
commands.description: Queries the oldest entry in the error queue (positive = instrument-specific, negative = SCPI). Returns 0,"No error" if empty. Identical to STATus:QUEue:NEXT?. Query only.

commands.name: system_klock
commands.description: Keyboard LOCK. Disables (ON) or enables (OFF) the keyboard including the LOCAL key. If SYSTem:SECurity is ON, KLOCk OFF is not accepted. *RST value OFF.

commands.name: system_mode
commands.description: Sets the instrument operating mode. FIXed: state switched only by *RCL; MSEQuence: instrument states from SYSTem:MSEQuence:RCL set successively. *RST value FIXed.

commands.name: system_msequence_catalog_query
commands.description: Queries the available memory sequences as a comma-separated list. Query only.

commands.name: system_msequence_select
commands.description: Selects (or creates) a memory sequence by name (arbitrary string up to 7 letters).

commands.name: system_msequence_rcl
commands.description: Transmits the list of instrument-state numbers (from *SAV) to be set successively via simulated *RCL in MSEQuence mode. Not influenced by *RST.

commands.name: system_msequence_dwell
commands.description: Transmits the dwell-time list for the selected memory sequence (one value applies to all states). Not influenced by *RST.

commands.name: system_msequence_mode
commands.description: Specifies how the memory sequence is processed. AUTO: each trigger one full cycle; STEP: each trigger one step. *RST value AUTO.

commands.name: system_preset
commands.description: Triggers an instrument reset (same effect as the RESET key or *RST). Triggers an event (no *RST value).

commands.name: system_protect_state
commands.description: Switches a protection level (suffix 1|2|3) on or off; ON disables the functions of that level (no password needed), OFF re-enables given the correct 6-digit password (level 1 password 123456). *RST has no effect.

commands.name: system_security_state
commands.description: Switches the security state on or off. When ON, DISPlay:ANNotation ON and SYSTem:KLOCk OFF cannot be executed; on ON->OFF transition all data except calibration data are deleted. Not influenced by *RST or *RCL.

commands.name: system_serror_query
commands.description: Returns a list of all errors present at the time of the query (corresponds to the ERROR page in manual control). Query only.

commands.name: system_version_query
commands.description: Returns the SCPI version number the instrument complies with (e.g. 1994.0). Query only.

commands.name: test_ram_query
commands.description: Triggers a test of the RAM (0 = pass). Query only.

commands.name: test_rom_query
commands.description: Triggers a test of the main memory (EEPROM) (0 = pass). Query only.

commands.name: test_battery_ram_query
commands.description: Triggers a test of the RAM battery voltage (should be >= 2.1 V). Query only.

commands.name: test_battery_xmem_query
commands.description: Triggers a test of the XMEM (memory extension) battery voltage (should be >= 2.1 V). Query only.

commands.name: trigger_sweep_immediate
commands.description: Immediately starts a sweep (level or frequency, depending on the active MODE). TRIG1 = RF generator, TRIG2 = LF gen 2. Triggers an event (no *RST value).

commands.name: trigger_sweep_source
commands.description: Specifies the sweep trigger source. AUTO (SCPI IMMediate): free-running; SINGle (SCPI BUS): triggered by TRIG:SWE:IMM or *TRG; EXTernal: triggered via EXT.TRIG socket or GET. *RST value SINGle.

commands.name: trigger_dm_immediate
commands.description: Immediately starts processing of the DM data list (basic modulations) or the action set with TACTion (complex modulations). Valid for TRIGger1 only. Triggers an event (no *RST value).

commands.name: trigger_dm_source
commands.description: Specifies the DM trigger events. AUTO: condition always met (list repeated); SINGle: triggered by TRIG:DM:IMM or *TRG (list once); EXTernal: external trigger. Valid for TRIGger1 only. *RST value AUTO.

commands.name: trigger_list_immediate
commands.description: Immediately starts processing of a list in LIST mode. Valid for TRIGger1 only. Triggers an event (no *RST value).

commands.name: trigger_list_source
commands.description: Specifies the LIST trigger source. AUTO: free-running (list restarted); SINGle: triggered by TRIG:LIST:IMM (list once); EXTernal: via EXT.TRIG socket or GET. *RST value SINGle.

commands.name: trigger_msequence_immediate
commands.description: Immediately starts a memory sequence. Valid for TRIGger1 only. Triggers an event (no *RST value).

commands.name: trigger_msequence_source
commands.description: Specifies the memory-sequence trigger source (cf. TRIGger:SWEep:SOURce). *RST value SINGle.

commands.name: trigger_pulse_source
commands.description: Specifies the pulse generator trigger source (option SM-B4, TRIGger1 only). EXTernal: via PULSE socket; AUTO: free-running. *RST value AUTO.

commands.name: trigger_pulse_slope
commands.description: Sets whether the pulse generator is triggered on the positive or negative edge of its trigger signal. *RST value POSitive.

commands.name: trigger_slope
commands.description: Sets the active edge of the external trigger input (acts on TRIGger:SWEep, :LIST and :MSEQuence). *RST value POSitive.

commands.name: unit_angle
commands.description: Specifies the default unit for angles when none is given in a command. *RST value RADian.

commands.name: unit_power
commands.description: Specifies the default unit for power/level when none is given in a command. *RST value DBM.

parameters.name: unit
parameters.range: DBM | DBW | DBMW | DBUW | DBV | DBMV | DBUV | V
